pages.text: One sunny afternoon, <name> was exploring the garden when he noticed a tiny caterpillar resting on a leaf. It looked pale and slow. “Oh no, are you sick?” <name> asked gently.
A wise old ladybug crawled nearby. “The little one is fine, just weak from growing,” she explained. “Soon it will spin a cocoon and transform into a butterfly.”

<name> wanted to help. He made a small safe spot on a branch, added some fresh leaves, and asked a chatty grasshopper about what caterpillars need. “Food, sunlight, and a quiet place,” the grasshopper chirped.

Every day, <name> checked on the caterpillar, learning about how it eats and grows. Slowly, it began spinning a silken cocoon around itself. “This is called metamorphosis,” the ladybug said. “Inside, the caterpillar changes completely into a butterfly.”

<name> waited patiently, amazed as days passed. Then one morning, the cocoon shook and split open. A bright, colorful butterfly stretched its wings and fluttered into the air. <name> clapped in delight.
“Wow,” he whispered. “The caterpillar grew and changed. That’s the life cycle!” <name> smiled, feeling proud that he helped the little creature safely through its magical transformation.
